title: A fan assembly
abstract: A fan assembly (100) comprising a nozzle having an opening (14) extending through the nozzle and one or more primary airflow outlets (15). The system further comprises an airflow generator (20) arranged to generate a primary airflow (29) for supply to the one or more primary airflow outlets, such that discharge of the primary airflow from the one or more primary airflow outlets is capable of drawing an external airflow (30) through the opening. Further, the fan assembly (100) is configured to selectively obstruct the external airflow through the opening.
claims:
Claims 1. A fan assembly comprising: a nozzle having an opening extending through the nozzle, and one or more primary airflow outlets; and an airflow generator arranged to generate a primary airflow for supply to the one or more primary airflow outlets, such that discharge of the primary airflow from the one or more primary airflow outlets is capable of drawing an external airflow through the opening; wherein the fan assembly is configured to selectively obstruct the external airflow through the opening. 2. A fan assembly according to claim 1, wherein the one or more primary airflow outlets are arranged to discharge the primary airflow away from the opening. 3. A fan assembly according to claim 2, wherein the nozzle comprises one or more secondary airflow outlets to discharge a secondary airflow into the opening. 4. A fan assembly according to claim 3, wherein the secondary airflow is supplied to the one or more secondary airflow outlets from the airflow generator. 5. A fan assembly according to claim 4, comprising an airflow director to adjust a proportion of the secondary airflow relative to the primary airflow. 6. A fan assembly according to any one of claims 3 to 5, wherein the opening comprises a central axis and the one or more secondary airflow outlets are arranged to discharge the secondary airflow at an angle to the central axis. 7. A fan assembly according to any one of claims 3 to 6, comprising a controller configured to control the fan assembly according to: 

 a high-entrainment mode wherein the secondary airflow comprises a first flow rate; and a low-entrainment mode wherein the secondary airflow comprises a second flow rate, the second flow rate being greater than the first flow rate. 8. A fan assembly according to claim 7, wherein the controller is configured to control the fan assembly in the high-entrainment mode such that the secondary airflow is negligible. 9. A fan assembly according to claim 7 or 8, wherein the controller is configured to control the fan assembly in the low-entrainment mode such that the primary airflow is negligible. 10. A fan assembly according to any one of the preceding claims, comprising a physical barrier to selectively physically obstruct the external airflow through the opening. 11. A fan assembly according to claim 10, wherein the physical barrier is releasably mountable to the nozzle. 12. A fan assembly according to claim 10 or 11, wherein the physical barrier is porous. 13. A fan assembly according to any one of claim 10 to 12, wherein the physical barrier is configurable between: a retracted position in which the opening is substantially less obstructed by the physical barrier; and an extended position in which the opening is more obstructed by the physical barrier. 14. A fan assembly according to any one of claims 11 to 13, wherein the physical barrier comprises a plurality of blades to collectively define a central aperture, and wherein the plurality of blades are moveable to vary a size of the central aperture. 15. A fan assembly according to any one of claims 3 to 9, wherein the one or more secondary airflow outlets are configured to selectively aerodynamically obstruct the external airflow through the opening. 16. A fan assembly according to claim 15, wherein the nozzle comprises one or more tertiary airflow outlets arranged to discharge air into the opening. 17. A fan assembly according to claim 16, wherein the one or more tertiary outlets are spaced from the one or more secondary outlets along the opening. 18. A fan assembly according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the one or more primary airflow outlets comprises one or more slots extending at least partially around a periphery of the opening. 19. A fan assembly according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein a cross-sectional area of the opening is greater than a total cross-sectional area of the one or more primary airflow outlets. 20. A fan assembly according to any one of the preceding claims, comprising an air treatment device arranged to treat the primary airflow before the primary airflow is discharged from the one or more primary airflow outlets. 21. A fan assembly according to claim 19, wherein the air treatment device comprises at least one of a heater, cooler, filter, humidifier, dehumidifier, ionizer, air purifier or device that adds or removes VOCs and/or VICs.
